title: Uwana Buyer
content:
{{Top|fa|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:UwanaBuyer.jpg]] |name=''Uwana Buyer'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]]{{GamerCite|4|Ships of the Smuggler's Alliance}} |line= |model=[[Luxury 5000]] |type=[[Realspace/Legends|Space]] [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] |cost=*1,000,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] (new) *500,000 credits (used) |modifier=[[Talon Karrde]] |sysmods= |length=65 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine=6 [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[Starchaser Mk. III]]s |hyperdrive=*Class 1 (primary) *Class 8 (back-up) |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*2 heavy [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] *1 [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] *1 [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] launcher |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=2; 3 [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] |skeleton= |passengers=16 |capacity=150 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] |cargohandling= |consumables=6 [[Standard month/Legends|months]] |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role=Personal yacht |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles= |affiliation=*[[Car'das/Legends|Talon Karrde's organization]] *[[Smugglers' Alliance]] |navy= |fleet= |owners=Talon Karrde |captains=Talon Karrde |crewmembers=*[[Quelev Tapper]]{{JournalCite|1|First Contact}} *[[Unidentified Twi'lek masseuse|Twi'lek masseuse]] |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|Oh, come now. Hart and Seoul? Not to mention a ship called the ''Uwana Buyer''? It was all just a little too cute; and I remembered hearing about a smuggler chief who had a fondness for cute wordplay.|Mara Jade, to Talon Karrde|}} The '''''Uwana Buyer''''' was a [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] [[Luxury 5000]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] owned and operated by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Chief/Legends|chief]] [[Talon Karrde]]. Heavily modified by Karrde to suit his needs, the ''Uwana Buyer'' was the most extravagant and luxurious [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] in Karrde's [[Car'das/Legends|fleet]]. He used it to impress potential clients whom he considered to be gullible enough to be amazed by wealth and riches, but rarely spent much [[Time/Legends|time]] aboard himself, preferring to operate from aboard the [[Action VI bulk transport/Legends|Action VI transport]] ''[[Wild Karrde]]''. Karrde operated the ''Uwana Buyer'' through the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], and he employed it as an incentive for his associates; those who served him well were assigned to crew the yacht to take advantage of its comforts. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Karrde and his Human second-in-command, [[Quelev Tapper]], took the ''Uwana Buyer'' to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]] to investigate the operations of a [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] named [[Gamgalon]]. During the mission, Tapper was [[Death/Legends|killed]], and Karrde saved by a Human [[Gender/Legends|woman]] named [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]. ==Description== {{Quote|At least… we got a… newer ship in the deal.''"
"''Yeah. She needs a new name, though.''"
"''How about the ''Uwana Buyer''?''"
"''Naaah. It's been done.|Smugglers [[Kaj Nedmak]] and [[Celia Durasha]] discuss the naming options for their new ship|{{JournalCite|14|Crimson Bounty}}}} The ''Uwana Buyer'' was a [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] [[Luxury 5000]] [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] owned by [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Chief/Legends|chief]] [[Talon Karrde]] which was worth 1,000,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] new, and a half-million credits used. A 65-[[Meter/Legends|meter]] elongated crescent with two forward sweeping mandibles, the Luxury 5000 was split into two decks. Karrde gutted the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] original interior and rebuilt the entire vessel to serve his purposes. He added extra cargo space along the curved bulkheads—enough to carry 150 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]]—and appointed the rest of the interior as opulently as a luxurious [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]]. Four staterooms dominated the lower level, as well as two crew rooms. On the port aft side, Karrde added a gourmet kitchen and dining room, and a well-appointed conference room. On the starboard aft side, an exercise room and a [[Medical bay/Legends|medical bay]] were added, as well as a fully equipped [[Spa/Legends|spa]] and massage room. The rear of the deck was dominated by two [[Engineer/Legends|engineering]] sections, and a small [[Casino/Legends|casino]]—which included two [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] tables and a bar—was located between the two. An aft lounge led directly off the casino. Three [[Entry ramp/Legends|boarding ramps]] served the deck; one for passenger boarding, and one on each of the forward mandibles designed for cargo. The ship's [[Airlock/Legends|airlocks]] were also located on the lower deck. The upper deck was smaller than the lower one. It also contained engineering sections at the rear, as well as the majority of the crew quarters and amenities, such as [[Refresher/Legends|refreshers]], a kitchen, and a galley. The [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] was at the fore of the deck, flanked by the gunwells to the forward mounted weapons. A single [[Turbolift/Legends|lift tube]] allowed access between the two decks. Karrde upgraded the ship's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] to a Class One, with a slower Class Eight as back-up, and replaced the original aging [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]] [[Engine/Legends|engines]] with six [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[Starchaser Mk. III]]s. Although Karrde intended to use the vessel as a place to meet and negotiate, he did not skimp on the weaponry, adding a pair of heavy [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] that flanked the bridge, an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]], and a single [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] launcher. The ''Uwana Buyer'' was nominally crewed by five people—a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and a co-pilot, as well as three [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]]. However, it could be handled by only two people when the need arose. It had room for sixteen passengers and could store enough consumables to last six [[Standard month/Legends|months]]. ==History== {{Quote|Welcome to Tropis-on-Varonat. Here for the sights?''"
"''That's very amusing. No, we're here for the hyperdrive mechanic we very much hope you have.''"
"''Ah. Yeah, I'm not surprised. The flashier the hull, the more crumbish the innards.|Gamgalon's employee [[Fleck (Human)|Fleck]] and Talon Karrde discuss the ''Uwana Buyer'' on Varonat|}} [[File:Uwana_Buyer_deck_plan.jpg|thumb|right|The deck plans of the ''Uwana Buyer'']] The most luxurious and extravagant ship in Talon Karrde's [[Car'das/Legends|fleet]], the ''Uwana Buyer'' was so named to satisfy Karrde's sense of wordplay when christening ships. Despite its luxury, Karrde spent little [[Time/Legends|time]] on the ship, preferring his office and quarters aboard one of his other vessels, the [[Action VI bulk transport/Legends|Action VI transport]] ''[[Wild Karrde]]''. The smuggler chief used the ''Uwana Buyer'' as a showroom, a place to take prospective clients that he felt were gullible enough to be dazzled by his wealth. In addition to that role, Karrde used the ship as a reward for his employees. Those associates who performed well for Karrde would be assigned to the ship so that their next voyage would be a comfortable one. Karrde operated the ''Uwana Buyer'' during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].In ''[[Crimson Bounty]]'', set during the Rebellion era, [[Kaj Nedmak]] suggests renaming their ship to the ''Uwana Buyer''. [[Celia Durasha]] mentions that "It's been done," indicating that the ''Uwana Buyer'' was active during the Rebellion era. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Karrde and his second-in-command, the Human [[Quelev Tapper]], took the ship to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]] on the [[Ison Trade Corridor/Legends|Ison Corridor]]. Karrde and Tapper were investigating the operation of a [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], [[Gamgalon]], on the planet, who was using a [[Morodin/Legends|Morodin]]-[[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] safari as a cover for illegal activities. Karrde, ever mindful of new business opportunities, wanted to see if Gamgalon's activities were something on which he could cut himself in. Posing as a representative of the [[Sif-Uwana Council]], Karrde had one of his associates, a Human named [[Chin]], gimmick the ''Uwana Buyer''{{'s}} hyperdrive by mis-aligning the [[Flux capacitor connector/Legends|power flux connector]] by four degrees. That gave Karrde and Tapper a legitimate excuse to land on Varonat, and while waiting for the hyperdrive to be fixed, participate in one of Gamgalon's safaris. During the investigation, Karrde's and Tapper's covers were blown, and Tapper was [[Death/Legends|killed]]. Karrde was saved by the hyperdrive [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] whom they had hired. Like Karrde, the Human woman was operating on Varonat under an assumed name. She had suspected Karrde was more than a bored, wealthy [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucrat]] upon hearing the name of his ship and the aliases that he and Tapper had used—[[Syndic/Legends|Syndic]] Pandis Hart and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Seoul, respectively. The mechanic, whom Karrde knew as "Celina Marniss," revealed her true name to Karrde as the pair escaped Varonat aboard the ''Uwana Buyer'': [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]. ==Commanders and crew== Talon Karrde owned the ''Uwana Buyer'', but preferred to travel aboard the ''Wild Karrde'', leaving the command and crewing of the ship to his associates. Quelev Tapper crewed the ship during the Varonat mission, and Karrde employed a full-time [[Unidentified Twi'lek masseuse|Twi'lek masseuse]] to run the on-board spa. She was a medical student paying her way through [[school]] by working aboard the ''Uwana Buyer'', and she doubled as the ship's [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] when the need arose. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Uwana Buyer'' first appeared in ''[[First Contact]]'', a short story written by [[Timothy Zahn]] for [[West End Games]]' [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]] in [[1994]]. ''First Contact'' was later reprinted in ''[[The Best of the Star Wars Adventure Journal, Issues 1-4]]'' in [[1996]], ''[[Tales from the Empire]]'' in [[1997]], and on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|''Hyperspace'']]. The ship was mentioned in ''[[Crimson Bounty]]'', published in 1997's [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 14|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 14]] by [[Charlene Newcomb]] and [[Rich Handley]]. The ''Uwana Buyer'' was mentioned in ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' in [[1995]], and was featured in the article ''[[Ships of the Smuggler's Alliance]]'', published in [[Star Wars Gamer 4|''Star Wars Gamer'' 4]] in [[2001]], which provided the first visual image of the ship by [[Jeffery Carlisle]] and expanded on its history. It later received a mention in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Uwana Buyer}} *{{JournalCite|14|Crimson Bounty}} {{Mo}} *{{JournalCite|1|First Contact|reprint=1}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{GamerCite|4|Ships of the Smuggler's Alliance}} {{1stp}} *{{FactFile|50|KAR 1-4|Talon Karrde}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Luxury 5000 yachts]] [[Category:Starships of the Car'das smugglers]] [[Category:Starships of the Smugglers' Alliance]]
